# Transcrição: 06-How to Set Up Reporting Columns 

- **Fonte:** 06-How to Set Up Reporting Columns 
- **Duração:** 03:49
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:03]

Okay, so when we log into Ads Manager, by default, Facebook's going to show you all these reporting columns, reach, results, impressions, etc., etc. Now this is possibly completely irrelevant to what you're needing to know. Remember we mapped out our funnel, right? We mapped out our client funnel and we also put the stats of where things should be converting. In order to really see where things are tracking or coming along, what we need to do is be able to pull that data from our ad account, right? And not only that, just in general, every time we log in, just be able to clearly identify issues by having all the data in front of us at any one time. So this is what we do by prepping the account and setting up our reporting columns. So what I'm going to do here is I'm going to click on this little column here and I'm going to click on Customize Columns. And I'm going to start to edit this column in a way that's going to make sense for me. So tracking along this funnel, all the steps. So for example here, I'm going to remove this, results. We want reach and impressions because we want to be able to see if there's been enough data. We're going to remove cost per result. Okay, I'm going to have my relevancy score. I'm going to put this down here with this. Frequency, link clicks. Okay, so here's what we want to add to this. So the first step of my funnel, the first event is viewed magnet. So I created a custom event of this. So I'm going to type bring this up in here. There it is. We're going to do cost per viewed magnet. And then the next step is lead. So people opting in. So I'm going to pull in my lead. And again, put this like this. And then the next one is viewed the sales page. So there we go. And then finally purchased. And there we go. So now I'll be able to see the frequency. I'll be able to see all the steps. The other stats that I want to add to this is click through rate. And I'm going to do link click. And I'm going to put this right over here.

## [03:03 - 03:49]

So I've got my link clicks, click through rate, frequency, relevancy score, impressions, reach, how much I've spent. And then I've got every step along my funnel as well as what the cost per result was for that. So I'm going to go ahead and save this as a preset. So rapid fire funnel, for example, and click apply. And now I can even set this as a default if I wanted to. So every time I log in, this is what I see. But now I can easily access it from this drop down every single time by clicking rapid fire funnel. I'll be able to see every step in my funnel get a really clear picture of where things are performing and the information that I need to look at to make a call that's going to work.
